title: Mental Health Evaluation * Domestic Violence Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Mental Health Evaluation * Domestic Violence Issues 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Adolescents Children Adolescents Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues AIDS/HIV Issues Children Domestic Violence Issues Grade School Students Hispanic/Latino Community Homelessness Issues Immigration Issues Infants/Toddlers LGBTQ2+ Issues Native American Community Older Adults Perinatal Depression Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES ALCOHOL AND DRUG COUNSELING AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health and substance abuse assessments. Specialists provide consultations, referrals, support and resources to staff, and group therapy, such as Seeking Safety. All consultations and assessments are confidential, except when there's a risk of harm to oneself or others, or in cases of child, disabled person, or elder abuse. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing the impacts of intimate partner violence and/or sexual assult Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES ALCOHOL AND DRUG COUNSELING AND ADVOCACY
